briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Wise under investigation over money laundering control concerns](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cy42x8ye02vo?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=rss)** — *BBC Business* — Wise is cooperating with the Brussels prosecutor's office amid an investigation into its anti-money laundering controls, though no specific findings have yet been shared with the company.

- **[UK banks blocked from cyber AI tool Mythos get offer from rival OpenAI](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cm2p3j6lvn7o?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=rss)** — *BBC Business* — Nine UK banks denied access to Anthropic's Mythos cybersecurity AI tool have been offered access to OpenAI's GPT 5.5 Cyber as an alternative.

- **[Goodbye Visa and Mastercard: 130M Europeans switching to sovereign payment](https://www.lesnumeriques.com/banque-en-ligne/adieu-visa-et-mastercard-130-millions-d-europeens-basculent-vers-un-paiement-100-souverain-des-2026-n250918.html)** — *Hacker News* — 130 million Europeans are set to transition to a sovereign payment network in 2026, reducing dependence on US card giants Visa and Mastercard.

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the bankruptcy of fintech middleware company Synapse, highlighting risks in the banking-as-a-service model.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Canada slipped into a technical recession on an annualized basis as economic growth stalled in 1st quarter](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/recession-gdp-may-2026-statscan-9.7216352?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canada entered a technical recession in Q1 2026 as economic growth stalled, raising pressure on financial institutions and fintech firms serving consumers under strain.

- **[Bank of Canada says financial system is in good shape, but vulnerabilities have increased](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/bank-of-canada-financial-stability-9.7215056?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — The Bank of Canada's latest financial stability assessment finds the system resilient but flags growing vulnerabilities that could affect credit markets and lending conditions.

- **[Questrade's Salim Naran doesn't understand why more people aren't using AI for personal finance](https://betakit.com/questrades-salim-naran-doesnt-understand-why-more-people-arent-using-ai-for-personal-finance/)** — *BetaKit* — Questrade's head of product argues that AI tools for personal finance are underutilized despite their potential to help Canadians make better financial decisions.

- **[BDC extends Isabelle Hudon's mandate as CEO through 2030](https://betakit.com/bdc-extends-isabelle-hudons-mandate-as-ceo-through-2030/)** — *BetaKit* — The Business Development Bank of Canada has renewed CEO Isabelle Hudon's mandate through 2030, signalling continuity in its support for Canadian entrepreneurs and startups including in the fintech space.

- **[Canada's Bill C-22 creates a blueprint for surveillance](https://betakit.com/canadas-bill-c-22-creates-a-blueprint-for-surveillance/)** — *BetaKit* — Critics warn that Canada's Bill C-22 establishes a framework with significant surveillance implications, raising data privacy concerns relevant to open banking and financial data regulation.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada's early commitment to phasing out screen scraping in favour of open banking APIs remains foundational context as the country continues to build out its Consumer-Driven Banking framework — directly relevant to Atlantic credit unions and fintechs preparing for implementation.

- **[Canadian B2B software firms are falling behind in agentic AI: report](https://betakit.com/canadian-b2b-software-firms-are-falling-behind-in-agentic-ai-report/)** — *BetaKit* — A new report finds Canadian B2B software companies are lagging in adopting agentic AI, a gap that Atlantic fintech startups and ecosystem builders should take note of as competition for embedded finance solutions intensifies.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — This analysis of how open banking reshapes payments competition offers valuable strategic insight for Atlantic Canadian credit unions and fintechs navigating Canada's evolving open banking landscape.
